info.title: Alert Management API
info.description: Alerts API for creating, updating, and querying for alerts.

get.description: Returns all metadata for active alerts in the requesters jurisdiction - paginated.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description:
Start date as a UNIX timestamp (in milliseconds). If set, only
records from after the start date will be returned, in ascending
order. If set to a value other than a positive integer, will be
ignored.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description:
End date as a UNIX timestamp (in milliseconds). If set, only
records from up to the end date will be returned, in ascending order
. If set to a value other than a positive integer, will be
ignored. A 400 will be returned if this is less than start.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of location records to return per page. The default is 10 and the maximum is 100. If set without `start`, will return results starting with the oldest available record. If set to a value other than a positive integer, will be ignored.

alerts_until.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of the created_time of the most recent record returned. This value should be used as the "start" parameter of the next request to implement pagination. This value will be 0 if no alerts were found.

get.description: Returns all metadata for active the specified alert.

parameters.name: alert_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the Alert to query the status of

parameters.name: extra
parameters.description: Comma seperated values that allow for different types of extra_data to be included. Currently only log_messages will be returned

patch.description:
**Please note the following limitations of this endpoint:**
1. Only Central Station integrations may set the following statuses: `DISPATCH_REQUESTED`, `IGNORED`, `CANCELED`
2. Only PSAP integrations may set the following statuses: `ACCEPTED`, `DECLINED`
3. Only PSAP integrations can set the `disposition` and `decline_reason` fields

parameters.name: alert_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the Alert to query the status of

400.description: Bad Request - Returned if passing invalid payload values

403.description: Forbidden - Returned when attempting to set a status you are not authorized to

409.description: Conflict - Returned when attempting to make an invalid status transition i.e. from `ACCEPTED` to `DECLINED`

get.description: Returns all log messages that are associated with an alert. The log messages are sorted by created_time.

parameters.name: alert_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the Alert to query log messages for.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The number of the page for which to return results, where page size is determined by limit param. If page is omitted the endpoint returns the first page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of log messages to retrieve.

post.description: Creates a log message for the alert

parameters.name: alert_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the Alert to to create log messages for.

ErrorResponse.description: Detail of the error response as a string

latitude.description: Latitude coordinate of the alert incident location.

longitude.description: Longitude coordinate of the alert incident location.

uncertainty_radius.description: Horizontal uncertainty in meters of the alert incident location provided by the location source device.

geodetic.description: Geodetic location information available for this incident

name.description: Name of the location i.e. business name

street_1.description: Street address of the nearest civic location to the geolocation

street_2.description: Additional street address information such as apartment number or floor.

city.description: City or locality of the civic location

state.description: ISO 3166-2 code of state or region of the civic location

zip_code.description: Zip/postal code of the civic location

civic.description: Civic location information available for this incident

Location.description:
Location of the Alert incident. When creating an alert, the client must provide one of `geodetic` or `civic`. 
In the event that both are provided `geodetic` will take precedence. If both are received, `geodetic` shall take precedence.

source_id.description: The ID assigned by the Central Monitoring Station for the originating service provider. This is a wholesale monitoring center's own internal ID for the customer facing alarm dealer.

incident_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of when the alert occurred.

service_provider_name.description: Customer facing service provider or brand known by end users. For wholesale monitoring stations, this would be the dealer's name.

description.description: Description providing details and context for what event the alert represents
description.example: Smoke detector tripped in zone 1 on floor 2.

AlertMetadataRequest.description: Core fields required for creating an Alert record.

AlertMetadataResponse.description: Core Alert data including the data provisioned by the Alert creator as well as the covering PSAP that would dispatch it and the RSOS ID info.

alert_id.description: Unique identifier of the specific alert.

last_updated_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of when the latest status change occurred

sla_expiration_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of when the Alert must be accepted by the PSAP by, otherwise it will be considered timed out and unserviceable via the Alerts platform.

dispatch_requested_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of when authorized entity (i.e. Central Station) requested dispatch from the covering PSAP

display_name.description: Human friendly name of the emergency type value

name.description:
Internal value of the site type. Residential and commercial alerts are 
typically fixed (civic) locations, while personal and vehicle 
alerts are typically GPS (geodetic) locations.

display_name.description: Human friendly name of the site type value

id.description: Unique RSOS account identifier of the PSAP with jurisdiction over this alert location.

name.description: Name of the PSAP with jurisdiction over this alert location.

phone.description: Administrative phone number of this PSAP

id.description: Unique RSOS account identifier of the authorized entity responsible for this alert.

name.description: Name of the authorized entity responsible for this alert.

phone.description: Administrative phone number of this authorized entity

followers.description: User IDs of all CSP and RSP users who have taken action on this alert (chat, status update, etc.)

supplemental_only.description: Flag indicating if this Alert is being used at the PSAP as supplemental data only. If `true` the PSAP cannot update status or chat and a traditional 10-digit adminstriative call is required for servicing the alert.

adr.description: Array of all ADR records related to this alert record

logs.description: Array of all log records related to this alert record

status.description: The name of the status to update the Alert to

disposition.description: Name of the disposition to update the Alert with

decline_reason.description: Descriptive message to provide details why the Alert was declined. This can only be set when the Alert is being set to, or in the `DECLINED` status

cancel_reason.description: Descriptive message to provide details why the Alert was canceled. This can only be set when the Alert is being set to, or in the `CANCELED` status

AlertStatusRequest.description: Alert status information to update. Must provide either `status` or `disposition` value to update.

name.description: Internal name of the Alert status

display_name.description: Human friendly name of the status value

name.description: Internal name of the Alert disposition

display_name.description: Human friendly name of the disposition value

disposition.description: Description providing further specficity on the current status of the alert

last_updated_time.description: Millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp of when the latest status change occurred

message.description: The chat message, as entered by the end user, that is to be broadcast to all chat participants.

sender.description: The full name of the user who sent the message.

sender_id.description: The database id of the user who sent the message.

LogMessageRequest.description: Contains data required to create a log message.

LogMessageResponse.description: Contains log message data related to an alert.

alert_id.description: The unique identifier for an alert event.

message.description: The chat message, as entered by the end user, that is to be broadcast to all chat participants.

sender.description: The full name of the user who sent the message.

sender_id.description: The database id of the user who sent the message.

entity_display_name.description: The name of the entity that generated the log message.

created_at.description: The millisecond-precise UNIX timestamp indicating when the alert record was stored in the database.

EmergencyTypeValues.description: The type of emergency event that this alert describes.
